name: away_natvent_not_activated
description: Away mode, warm day: outdoor 68F is cooler than indoor 74F, so nat-vent would be favorable IF someone were home. A window opens at 14:00 while the home is empty. Issue #254: nat-vent must NOT activate in an unoccupied home — the open contact pauses HVAC instead of running the fan in an empty house.
notes: Occupant outcome: a person who leaves on a warm day with a window inadvertently left open does NOT come home to a fan that ran all afternoon in an empty house. The open contact pauses HVAC instead.

Issue #254 added an occupancy guard to nat-vent activation (handle_door_window_open + the two check_natural_vent_conditions re-entry paths). #231 fixed the away nat-vent EXIT; this guards the activation.
Regression proof: remove the occupancy guard and the 14:00 assertion flips from 'paused' to 'natural_ventilation' (the fan activates in the empty home).

events.note: Afternoon: indoor 74F, outdoor 68F — nat-vent eligible by temperature alone (outdoor < indoor, indoor > comfort_heat)
Window opens while away — occupancy guard suppresses nat-vent; the normal HVAC pause fires instead

assertions.reason: Issue #254: sensor_open while away — the occupancy guard suppresses nat-vent activation; the normal door/window HVAC pause fires instead of running the fan in an empty home.

verdict.summary: Away mode suppresses nat-vent activation — an open contact in an empty home pauses HVAC, it does not run the fan
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied -> away setback (79F) -> paused on sensor_open at 14:00 (no nat-vent)
verdict.expected_behavior: Nat-vent activates only when occupancy is home or guest; away/vacation forces the pause path (Issue #254)
